title: open_swtpm env mutation is not thread-safe: POSIX race on environ
description: In wolftpm/src/device.rs Device::open_swtpm (lines 46-63) and wolftpm-tss/src/connection.rs WolfTpmSwtpm::connect (lines 158-177), the code calls setenv() to set SWTPM_SERVER_NAME and SWTPM_SERVER_PORT, calls wolfTPM2_Init, then calls unsetenv(). POSIX (Austin Group issue 0000547) specifies that calling setenv/unsetenv concurrently with any other function that reads the environment (getenv, putenv, setenv, exec*, or even getenv calls inside wolfTPM2_Init on another thread) is undefined behavior in a multi-threaded process. Because Rust programs are multi-threaded by default (tokio, rayon, std threads), this sequence is a data race on the environ global. Additionally two concurrent calls to open_swtpm with different host/port will corrupt each other's environment. Fix: protect the setenv/wolfTPM2_Init/unsetenv sequence with a process-global Mutex, or pass host/port via a wolfTPM ioCb user context instead of environment variables.
